REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS COMPLAINANT JAMES PERRY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE DEBORAH AND JAMES PERRY REVOCABLE TRUST, DEBORAH PERRY MOTION NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF PARTITION, APPOINTMENT OF APPRAISER AND BROKER PURSUANT TO THE PARTITION OF REAL PROPERTY ACT; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT THEREOF; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE; DECLARATIONS OF SCOTT TALKOV, JAMES PERRY, DEBORAH PERRY, MATTHEW TAYLOR IS GRANTED, IN PART. THE COURT ORDERS A PARTITION BY OPEN-MARKET SALE OF THE PROPERTY, PURSUANT TO SECTION 874.320. THE PARTIES HAVE 10 DAYS AFTER THE ENTRY OF ORDER TO AGREE ON A REAL ESTATE BROKER LICENSED IN THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA TO OFFER THE PROPERTY FOR SALE. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT COME TO AN AGREEMENT, THE COURT SHALL APPOINT THE BROKER AND ESTABLISH A REASONABLE COMMISSION. THE COURT NOTES THAT MR. MICHAEL BELLINGS, THE PARTIES PROPOSED LISTING AGENT, IS NOT A BROKER. THE COURT WILL SET A STATUS CONFERENCE ON APRIL 21, 2025 TO APPOINT A BROKER IF THE PARTIES CANNOT COME TO AN AGREEMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2026 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-22-2026 Continued To May-06-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion, And Motion, To Confirm Sale, And To Approve Broker'S Report
Off Calendar Oct-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Eli Underwood, Esq. representing Plaintiff Linda L. Perry who was not present.
Andrew Wiegel, Esq. representing Defendant James A. Perry who was present via Zoom Video Conference.
Vincent Malta, court appointed broker/ receiver
Allison Chaplean and Natalie Meyers, co-agents for Buyer Hans Geiszler.
Tom Benoit and Ben Klugman, Realtors for Buyer/Bidder Scott Lebus.
Vincent Heung, broker for Buyers/Bidders Dalong Luo and Hui Kuang Luo.

No attorneys present at the calling of the matter.
Mr. Malta represents that attorneys from both parties were not planning to appear based on the tentative ruling but also informed the court that he will get in touch with Plaintiff's counsel.
Mr. Underwood upon connecting to the Zoom Video Conference of the department confirms Mr. Malta's representation.

The Court informs Plaintiff's counsel that the court is unable to confirm the sale without all parties present.

Court takes a brief recess.
Proceedings resume.
Matter is re-called and all individuals present at court make their appearance.

The court instructs Mr. Malta as court appointed broker/receiver to state the terms of the sale of the property before the bidding starts.

Bidding starts at $1,313,000.00
The parties engage in the overbid process with the final bid made in the amount of $1,423,000.00 by Mr. Heung for Dalong Luo and Hui Kuang Luo.

The sale was confirmed to Mr. Heung as broker to Dalong Luo and Hui Kuang Luo in the amount of $1,423,000.00.

Mr. Heung is to provide his contact information to plaintiff's counsel and place his buyers' offer in writing based on the confirmed price.

As a courtesy, the Clerk sent Mr. Heung's contact information electronically to Plaintiff's counsel and other relevant parties.

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the court confirming the sale of the property that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-14-2025 Continued To Feb-04-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion, And Motion, To Compel Deborah Perry'S Further Responses To Discovery, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - PLAINTIFF LINDA PERRY's Motion, To Compel Deborah Perry'S Further Responses To Discovery, And For Sanctions.

Plaintiff's motion to compel further discovery responses from James Perry is denied.

First, stacked discovery motions that seek to compel further responses to special interrogatories, form interrogatories, further production, and further responses to the requests for admission are disfavored. Second, plaintiff's separate statements do not comply with CRC 3.1345. CRC 3.1345(c) states that "the separate statement must be full and complete so that no person is required to review any other document in order to determine the full request and the full response." Here, plaintiff's separate statements solely cite defendant's boilerplate objections and exclude the substantive aspects of defendant's responses. (See, e.g., Separate Statement Response to Form Rogs. 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, etc, and Ramirez Decl., Ex. 7; Separate Statement Response to Special Rogs. 2, 3, 7, etc. and Ramirez Decl., Ex. 8; Separate Statement Response to Request for Admission 3, 4, etc. and Ramirez Decl., Ex. 9; Separate Statement Response to Request for Production 2, 3, etc. and Ramirez Decl., Ex. 10.) The separate statements delete the complete responses, are misleading, and do not aid the court. Lastly, defendant provided supplemental responses after this motion was filed that moots the motion and plaintiff's reply does not highlight any existing discovery dispute other than sanctions. (Grosch Decl., pars. 2-4.)

The record does not show that either Plaintiff or Defendant James acted with substantial justification in substantial part with respect to this discovery. Plaintiff and Defendant, each, shall pay $125 as sanctions, payable to the court by way of the clerk of the court (a copy of this order to accompany payment), payment no later than by June 17, 2025, receipt filed no later than June 23, 2025.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order setting out the above text verbatim.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition, Appointment Of Appraiser And Broker Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Request For Judicial Notice; Declarations Of Scott Talkov, James Perry, Deborah Perry, Matthew Taylor
Off Calendar Apr-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT JAMES PERRY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE DEBORAH AND JAMES PERRY REVOCABLE TRUST, DEBORAH PERRY MOTION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition, Appointment Of Appraiser And Broker Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Request For Judicial Notice; Declarations Of Scott Talkov, James Perry, Deborah Perry, Matthew Taylor is GRANTED, in part. The Court orders a Partition by open-market sale of the property, pursuant to Section 874.320. The parties have 10 days after the entry of order to agree on a real estate broker licensed in the State of California to offer the property for sale. If the parties cannot come to an agreement, the court shall appoint the broker and establish a reasonable commission. The Court notes that Mr. Michael Bellings, the parties proposed listing agent, is not a broker. The Court will set a status conference on April 21, 2025 to appoint a broker if the parties cannot come to an agreement.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
